author: @WileHeCoyote
text:
I truly appreciate you taking the time to help guid your viewers Robert!  I've been meaning to simplify my life and reduce my average number of simultaneous experiments and projects, 10 fold! in order to focus on my true goals and dreams. and I WILL!

 With it reduced to only 5 simultaneous projects lol, I may even be able to figure out what and where my "train station" is! :)   
I do feel often as though I'm wandering aimlessly in the darkness, listening for a greek string of hope. A bit dramatic since I am actually very fortunate right now to have many chords in life that give me hope, guidance and vibrance!  

Your videos are among the most beautiful sounding strings in the song tho mate! they mean more than I can express. thank you very much good Sir Robert for your videos and I will always be listening if you have a lesson to teach.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: That's an awesome post mate - thank you for taking the time to write it - it does sound like you need a little more focus lol

author: @RWBHere
text: Thanks Robert. It's a salutary reminder. We all fall foul of that trap sooner or later. Confusion leads to procrastination and to missing the vital clues. This is why an independent auditor; a good 'critical friend', is needed. It's the reason why a young child can sometimes beat a chess master.

author: @markhenne1444
text: 2017 07 05 1904 Lava: Notice that Lava is a very radio active material. That is what allows it to maintain it's high temperature, fluid form, produce hyrogen and helium and other gases as it reaches the surface of this where gasses can escape, Like all residual earth material, lava is proceeding to decompress, as large Ions decompress and fission they are left with a smaller number of protons, since we classify atoms by their number of protons, we give names to material that is still decompressing. This leads to the understanding of why earth quakes happens, the earth expands, and divulges where earth came from and its ultimate demise and reprocessing in a very long term project.

author: @johnwilson3668
text:
Hi Robert
I see everything in the universe as simple because every thing that has happened is by chance and people seem to overcomplicate everything  so answers cannot be found. I make a few inventions and some of them
are crackpot but they work .There was a saying by Wernher von Braun "You are a crackpot until
you get the jackpot" .You remind me of Leonardo da Vinci. You think of an Idea and this  spawns another idea .Your destiny is to keep inventing all the time, not to manufacture them but carry on with your next idea .I personally
thought of an brilliant idea ,I didn't write it down .I carried on with the job that spawned  it ,then forgot the brilliant  Idea
only to remember it two years later when it came from nowhere back in my mind .You are meant to keep on inventing not to have you nitpicking though past inventions.How many of Leonardo's  ideas were lost by people giving him earache  
You have inspired me  and simpler the better

author: @Allwestconstruction
text:
An interesting article 
Scientists explain ancient Rome's long-lasting concrete

Ancient sea walls built by the Romans used a concrete made from lime and volcanic ash to bind with rocks.

Rather than eroding, particularly in the presence of sea water, the material seems to gain strength from the exposure.
In previous tests with samples from ancient Roman sea walls and harbours, researchers learned that the concrete contained a rare mineral called aluminium tobermorite.

replies.author: @GoogleMapsVids-Asia
replies.text: Steve Allwest I often wonder if the basic materials are not being observed. I the old days binders were very simple. But to with stand time I believe a product of non watering must have been used. Grease. Tree pitch. Simple stuff

replies.author: @Allwestconstruction
replies.text:
Yes, agree, sometimes making a product more complex is just making it more complex, not really better 

Although it is fun to make things more complex, need to fight the urge Lol

Oh and by the way, they are making binders out of tree pitch right now, decomposed granite with tree pitch makes for a semisolid pathway material

author: @Allwestconstruction
text:
In construction we use an acronym "KISS" ( Keep it simple stupid)

It always works out best to KISS what ever you are working on, it is easy to get caught up in complex ideas to get something done (because it is fun) but for production purposes it is always best to Kiss it 

Cheers

author: @ACTSRevolution
text: Robert, do you use a favorite laboratory notebook or capture method?  Have you thought of capture improvements that you would like, even if you feel you personally can get by without making the changes?  Many of us (viewers/fans) are not retired, and have little personal resources to keep our minds straight -- being employed, we cannot afford to get our lives any more confused and any physical tools you know of to fight this battle would be pure gold for the individual (and isolated, usually) project manager!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am a bit old school mate and keep journals - mine look like they came from a Dr Who set lol

replies.author: @ACTSRevolution
replies.text: U have a lot in the attic to keep organized and at the ready!  Many posters fancy the internet a treasure trove of usable miscellany - if only they could plot their progress to the most economic starting point.  I pursue this also - I even DL most of your vids, and a few others, along with these great comment sections!  Every January I print out the year's monthly calendars onto card stock and cut cut it up into 3x5s, including Project section and a Commitments section, and shopping lists go on the back.  As aFT engineer, a month of personal projects fits on a shirt-pocket sized card (sadly.)  The point is: if you miss the instant you have an insight, you dirty the pure motivation to pursue it, slowing further inspiration.  (Always keep a pocket journal!)

replies.author: @ACTSRevolution
replies.text: I also get many of those engineering grid notepads technical vendors give away whenever I go to a product expo, 'cuz the 8x11" tablets are too nice and pricey to use as scratch paper, but writing pads aren't good for sketches.  The important thing is: if a picture can be drawn, even if only a figurative scheme or block chart applies, always draw one, as this is your only reliable map for logistics and further idea development.  Planning a set of hard drive directories also applies here, since the internet can take a lifetime to get meaningful info from without a plan.  BTW, your megafarad capacitor update is one of my all-time favorite videos!  Thank you SO MUCH for not leaving us banging our heads in the dark!

author: @MerwinARTist
text:
Greetings Robert .. you are a breath of fresh air in a polluted world.  KISS .. Keep It Simple Sweetie ... I like the positive word at the end .. some people say "stupid" .. lol  

You are a wonderful teacher .. I've enjoyed watching some of your videos.  I want to recommend that you also check out Gerard Morin's channel.  Gerard has discovered some amazing things through his questioning process and more good minds need to be aware.  He's a bit brash with his mouth .. but I like his general honesty and character.

He has discovered some interesting things about how electricity works coming to our homes .. and is in the process of open sourcing his information.  See some of his other videos to understand how he has arrived at his present understanding.  

This is a link to his last video.  
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8NXcf_tX84w&feature=push-u-sub&attr_tag=9ZDiQovth6teme9T-6
